Always use the CPU Installation Tool, holding both sides firmly. Do not bend or damage the tool. Close the load lever to lock the CPU in place. <PIC>", ["Manual25_11"]

"This motherboard has four DDR4 DIMM sockets (288-pin). It supports 2 GB, 4 GB, 8 GB, and 16 GB unbuffered non-ECC DDR4 modules. DDR4 is notched differently from DDR/DDR2/DDR3, so only DDR4 modules can be installed. Keep DIMM voltage below 1.65V to protect the CPU. You can check the recommended memory population layout in the diagram below. <PIC> For dual-channel config, install matched modules in the recommended slots. Note that on a 32-bit OS, 4 GB or more installed memory may only show ~3 GB usable — a 64-bit OS is recommended if you need full memory capacity.", ["Manual25_17"]
"Serial port connector (10-1 pin COM) 是主板上的一个内部接口，用于连接串行（COM）端口模块。使用时，将串行端口模块的排线连接到该接口，再将模块安装到机箱背部的扩展槽开口处。<PIC> 需要注意的是，COM 模块需单独购买，不随主板附带。", ["Manual25_39"]

"The TPM connector (14-1 pin TPM) supports a Trusted Platform Module (TPM) system. It securely stores keys, digital certificates, passwords, and data. A TPM system also helps enhance network security, protects digital identities, and ensures platform integrity. <PIC>", ["Manual25_40"]

The Thermal sensor connector (2-pin T_SENSOR) is a connector on the motherboard that allows you to connect an external thermal sensor cable. It is used to monitor the temperature of a specific component or area inside the chassis that you want to keep track of. By attaching a thermal sensor to this connector, the system can detect and report the temperature from that location, helping with thermal management and system monitoring.

"For the multi-use pressure cooker, quick release (QR) works like this: press the quick release button down until it clicks and locks into the Vent position. <PIC> A jet of steam will eject through the top of the steam release valve — that's normal. Once pressure is fully released, the float valve drops into the lid. Avoid using QR with fatty, oily, thick, or high-starch foods, or foods that expand when cooked. For the multi-use air fryer lid, there is no quick release function — it does not use pressure. To pause or stop cooking, simply lift the air fryer lid off the cooker base and place it on the protective pad provided. Lifting the lid automatically pauses cooking.", ["Manual30_12"]

To set up the pressure cooking lid, first make sure the inner pot is clean and properly inserted into the cooker base. Inspect the lid carefully to ensure it is clean and in good working condition. Only use the authorized pressure cooking lid designed for this model — using any other lid may cause injury or damage. Before placing the lid, ensure the heating element is clean and dry. Add your ingredients and the required water-based liquid (at least enough for pressure to build). Once the lid is secured, select Pressure Cook or Steam from the Smart Programs, adjust time and pressure level as needed, then press Start. Do not open the lid until all internal pressure has been fully released — if the float valve is still up or the lid feels difficult to open, the appliance is still pressurized. Never force it open.

"The float valve is located on the pressure cooking lid and works as a visual pressure indicator. To install it: drop the narrow end of the float valve into the float valve hole on the top of the lid. <PIC> Place one finger on the flat top of the float valve, then turn the lid over. Firmly attach the silicone cap to the bottom of the float valve. <PIC> Once installed, the float valve shows two states: pressurized means it has visibly popped up above the lid surface <PIC>, and depressurized means it has descended flush with the lid. Never operate the cooker without the float valve and silicone cap properly installed, and do not touch the float valve during use.", ["Manual30_36", "Manual30_37", "Manual30_16"]

"The anti-block shield is a component on the pressure cooking lid that prevents food particles from entering the steam release pipe, helping regulate pressure safely. It is essential for product safety and must always be installed before pressure cooking. To remove it, grip the lid like a steering wheel and use your thumbs to press firmly against the side of the anti-block shield (pressing toward the side of the lid and upward) until it pops off the prongs underneath. <PIC> To reinstall, place it over the prongs and press down until it snaps into position. <PIC> For maintenance, check the anti-block shield before each use for any clogging. It should be removed, cleaned, and reinstalled as part of regular care. Never operate the pressure cooker without the anti-block shield installed.", ["Manual30_34", "Manual30_35"]

"The steam release valve sits loosely on the steam release pipe on top of the pressure cooking lid. To install it, place the valve onto the steam release pipe and press down firmly — it should remain in place when the lid is turned over, even though it sits loosely. <PIC> Never cover or block the steam release valve. It must be installed before pressure cooking. The quick release button controls the steam release valve. To vent pressure, press the quick release button down until it clicks and locks into the Vent position — a jet of steam will eject from the top of the valve, which is normal. <PIC>", ["Manual30_33", "Manual30_14"]

"To install the float valve, drop the narrow end into the float valve hole on the top of the lid. <PIC> Place one finger on the flat top of the float valve, then turn the lid over. Firmly attach the silicone cap to the bottom of the float valve. <PIC> The float valve and silicone cap work together to seal in pressurized steam and must both be installed before use. Do not operate the cooker without them properly installed.", ["Manual30_36", "Manual30_37"]

"The condensation collector installation is the same for both pressure cooker and air fryer modes. Align the grooves on the condensation collector over the tabs on the back of the cooker base, then slide it into place. <PIC> It sits at the back of the cooker base and catches overflow from the condensation rim. Always install it before cooking, then empty and rinse it after each use.", ["Manual30_38"]

"To install the sealing ring for the pressure cooker function, place the sealing ring over the sealing ring rack on the pressure cooker lid and press it firmly into place, pressing down to prevent puckering. When installed correctly, the ring sits snug behind the rack and will not fall out when the lid is turned over. <PIC> The ring is easiest to install when wet and soapy. For the air fryer function, the air fryer lid uses a separate lid design — it does not use a sealing ring. Simply line up the lid fins with the base handles and press down to ensure the power and sensor connectors are fully connected. <PIC> Always use only authorized sealing rings, and replace them every 12-18 months or sooner if stretched or damaged.", ["Manual30_31", "Manual30_26"]

"Grip the edge of the silicone and pull the sealing ring out from behind the circular stainless-steel sealing ring rack. <PIC> The sealing ring is easiest to remove when it is wet and soapy. After removal, inspect the steel rack to make sure it is secured, centered, and at an even height all the way around the lid. Do not attempt to repair a deformed sealing ring rack.", ["Manual30_30"]

"The sealing ring is a silicone ring that fits behind the stainless-steel sealing ring rack on the pressure cooker lid. <PIC> It functions by creating an airtight seal during pressure cooking, preventing steam from escaping and allowing pressure to build inside the pot. Only authorized sealing rings should be used. Always check for cuts, deformation, and correct installation before cooking. Sealing rings stretch over time and should be replaced every 12-18 months, or sooner if stretching, deformation, or damage is noticed. To install, place the sealing ring over the rack and press firmly to prevent puckering. When properly installed, it sits snug behind the rack and will not fall out when the lid is turned over. <PIC>", ["Manual30_16", "Manual30_31"]

"The steam release valve sits loosely on the steam release pipe on top of the pressure cooking lid. <PIC> Its job is to control whether pressure is held inside or released — it works together with the quick release button, which you press down to trigger venting. When activated, a jet of steam ejects from the top of the valve until pressure is fully released, at which point the float valve drops back into the lid. <PIC> The valve must always be installed before pressure cooking. Never cover or block it, as it is critical for safety. To install, simply place it on the steam release pipe and press down firmly — it sits loosely but stays in place when the lid is turned over.", ["Manual30_15", "Manual30_33"]

"The quick release button controls the steam release valve, which determines whether pressure is released or not from the pressure cooking lid. <PIC> It stops cooking quickly and prevents overcooking, making it ideal for quick-cooking vegetables and delicate seafood. To use it, press the quick release button down until it clicks and locks into the Vent position. <PIC> Note that the motion is more of a flick — don't turn it more than 45°, and let it spring back to its original position. When pressed, a jet of steam will eject through the top of the steam release valve — this is normal. Once pressure is fully released, the float valve drops into the lid.", ["Manual30_11", "multi-use_pressure_cooker_and_air_fryer_02"]

"Natural Release (NR or NPR) means cooking stops gradually. As the temperature inside the cooker drops, the cooker depressurizes on its own over time — no action needed from you. <PIC> Use NR when cooking high-starch foods like soups, stews, chilis, pasta, oatmeal, and congee, or foods that expand during cooking such as beans and grains.", ["Manual30_9"]

"The vacuum consists of the main robot body with a CLEAN button on top that illuminates during operation, a dust bin that can be removed and emptied, a replaceable filter, and a rechargeable battery pack. It features sensors to detect debris concentration and navigate around obstacles. The robot also works with a Home Base charging dock. Key external components include the CLEAN button, SPOT button, DOCK button, and a battery indicator light. <PIC>", ["Manual32_0"]

"The dual mode virtual wall barrier offers two modes: Halo Mode and Virtual Wall Mode. In Halo Mode (switch in the down position), the device creates a circular protective zone approximately 24 inches from its center, preventing the vacuum from bumping into fragile items like a dog bowl or vase, or drifting into unwanted spots like corners or under a desk. <PIC> In Virtual Wall Mode (switch in the up position), the device projects an invisible cone-shaped barrier that blocks openings up to 10 feet wide, acting as a boundary wall to keep the vacuum within a specific area. <PIC> Together, these modes let you customize cleaning zones to match different areas and needs in your home.", ["Manual32_5", "Manual32_6"]

"Press the bin release button to remove the bin from the vacuum. <PIC> Once removed, open the bin door to empty the contents into a trash container. <PIC> If the full bin indicator lights up during a cleaning job, you can pause the job, empty the bin, and then resume. If the indicator is on but the bin doesn't appear full, the full bin sensors may need cleaning.", ["Manual32_7", "Manual32_8"]

"Remove and empty the bin first. <PIC> Then wipe the sensors on the vacuum with a clean, dry cloth. <PIC> Finally, wipe the inner and outer sensor ports on the bin with a clean, dry cloth. <PIC>", ["Manual32_8", "Manual32_9", "Manual32_10"]

"To clean the extractors on your vacuum, start by pinching the yellow extractor frame release tabs, lift up the extractor frame, and remove any obstructions. <PIC> Next, remove the extractors and take off the yellow extractor caps. Clear any hair or debris collected under the caps and around the metal axles, then reinstall the caps. <PIC> For the square and hexagonal plastic pegs on the other side of the extractors, remove any hair and debris from those as well. <PIC> Then clear the vacuum path. <PIC> Finally, reinstall the extractors by matching the color and shape of the extractor pegs with the corresponding icons on the cleaning head module. The brush extractor and roller extractor follow the same procedure — both are removed together as part of the extractor set and cleaned the same way.", ["Manual32_15", "Manual32_16", "Manual32_17", "Manual32_18"]

"To clean the sensors on a vacuum cleaner, use a cloth dampened with water only to clean the camera sensor. Do not use any other cleaning agents or liquids. <PIC> To clean the charging contacts, first disconnect the power supply, then wipe the contacts with a damp cloth.", ["Manual32_0"]

Place the Home Base in an open, uncluttered area with at least 1.5 feet on each side, at least 4 feet in front of it, and at least 4 feet away from stairs. Keep it at least 8 feet from any virtual wall barriers. Always keep it plugged in and positioned in an area with consistent Wi-Fi coverage so the vacuum can receive information via the app.

"Pull firmly on the front caster wheel to remove it. <PIC> Remove any debris from inside the wheel cavity. Spin the wheel by hand — if rotation is restricted, remove the wheel from its housing and push firmly to remove the axle, then clear any debris or hair wrapped around it. Reinstall all parts when finished and make sure the wheel clicks back into place.", ["Manual32_11"]
"Use a coin or small screwdriver to remove the screw securing the side brush. <PIC> Remove the brush, clean the brush and the brush post, then reinstall the brush.", ["Manual32_13"]

"The dual-mode virtual wall barrier keeps the vacuum in areas you want cleaned and out of areas you don't. It has two modes: Virtual Wall Mode (switch up) creates an invisible cone-shaped barrier blocking openings up to 10 feet wide — the barrier widens with distance from the device. <PIC> Halo Mode (switch down) creates a circular protective zone approximately 24 inches from the device's center, preventing the vacuum from bumping into objects like a dog bowl or vase, or entering unwanted spots like corners. <PIC> Under normal use, batteries last 8–10 months. If storing the device long-term, switch it to the Off (middle) position.", ["Manual32_5", "Manual32_6"]

To power on the camera, turn the power switch to the ON position. The camera will activate and be ready to operate. Note that the camera turns off automatically after 1 minute of non-operation to save battery. To turn it back on, simply press the shutter button.

"Brake lever: Squeeze the brake lever to stop the machine. To release the parking brake, simply squeeze the brake lever again. Make sure the brake lever end does not project out over the handlebar end to prevent damage if the machine is placed on its side. Always test the brake at slow speed when starting out to confirm it works properly. <PIC> Parking brake button: To apply the parking brake, squeeze the brake lever first, then push down the parking brake button while releasing the brake lever — this locks it in place. <PIC> Never run the machine with the parking brake applied, as it may overheat the brake disc and reduce braking ability. Always set the parking brake before starting the engine.", ["Manual34_130", "Manual34_40"]

"Before operating a snowmobile, perform the following pre-use safety checks. Read the Owner's Manual and familiarize yourself with all controls. <PIC> Check throttle, steering, and brake operation — ensure the throttle lever moves freely and returns to idle when released. Apply the parking brake before starting the engine, but never ride with it applied. Check the drive track for damage or misadjustment; do not operate if damage is found. <PIC> Make sure no one is standing behind the machine during inspection or startup, as a broken track or debris can cause injury. Avoid operating after consuming alcohol or drugs. Confirm the machine will only be used on snow or ice, not dirt, sand, rocks, or pavement.", ["snowmobile_01", "snowmobile_02"]

"The V-Belt Holder is a storage compartment on the machine designed to hold a spare V-belt for emergency use. <PIC> To install a spare belt, loosen bolt ① to place the V-belt into the holder, then tighten the bolt securely. Make sure the fuel hose is not pinched during installation. <PIC> The V-belt must be secured firmly in the holder at all times, as a loose belt could contact the hot muffler and get damaged.", ["Manual34_51", "Manual34_52"]

"First, make sure the engine idle speed is adjusted before proceeding. Loosen the adjuster lock nut. <PIC> Turn the adjuster in or out until the proper throttle lever free play is achieved. Then tighten the locknut to secure the adjustment. Note that the locknut is marked ② and the adjuster is marked ③.", ["Manual34_148"]

"To steer a snowmobile, use 'body English' as the key technique. As you approach a curve, slow down first, then turn the handlebars in the desired direction. At the same time, shift your weight onto the running board on the inside of the turn and lean your upper body into the turn. <PIC> Practice this at slow speeds in a large flat area free of obstacles before attempting tighter or faster turns. Lean more as the turn gets sharper or at higher speeds. If the snowmobile begins to tip while turning, lean further into the turn to regain balance, and if needed, gradually ease off the throttle or steer toward the outside of the turn. On ice or hard-packed snow, steering traction is reduced, so avoid rapid turning and drive slowly and cautiously.", ["Manual34_127"]

"To turn a snowmobile, slow down as you approach the curve, then turn the handlebars in the desired direction. At the same time, shift your weight onto the running board on the inside of the turn and lean your upper body into the turn. <PIC> Practice this at slow speeds in a large flat area first. If the snowmobile begins to tip while turning, lean more into the turn to regain balance, or gradually release the throttle and steer toward the outside of the turn. Lean more as the turn gets sharper or at higher speeds.", ["Manual34_127"]

"Riding uphill requires preparation and proper technique. Practice on gentle slopes first before attempting steeper climbs. As you approach a hill, accelerate before starting the climb, then reduce throttle to prevent track slippage. Keep your weight on the uphill side at all times — lean forward on the handlebars, and on steeper inclines, stand on the running boards while leaning forward over the handlebars. <PIC> Slow down as you reach the crest, and be ready for obstacles, sharp drops, or other vehicles on the other side. If you cannot continue up the hill, do not spin the track. Stop the engine, set the parking brake, then pull the rear of the snowmobile around to point it back downhill. Never stand on the downhill side of the machine. Once pointed downhill, restart the engine, release the parking brake, and descend.", ["Manual34_127"]
"下坡时需将速度控制在最低限度。保持适当油门以使离合器处于接合状态，这样可以利用发动机压缩力辅助减速，防止雪地摩托自由滑行。<PIC> 同时频繁轻踩刹车配合减速。切勿过度制动，否则履带会锁死导致失控。", ["Manual34_128"]

"Crossing a slope requires proper weight positioning to maintain balance. As you travel across the slope, lean your body toward the uphill side. The recommended riding position is to kneel with the downhill leg's knee on the seat and the uphill leg's foot on the running board. <PIC> This makes it easier to shift your body weight as needed. Since snow and ice are slippery, be prepared for the snowmobile to slip sideways. If it does, steer in the direction of the slide if no obstacles are in your path, then gradually steer back in your intended direction. If the snowmobile starts to tip, steer downhill to regain balance. If tipping is unavoidable, dismount immediately on the uphill side. Side hills are not recommended for beginners or novice riders.", ["Manual34_129"]

"Remove the spark plug periodically to inspect it. Check the coloration on the white porcelain insulator around the center electrode. The ideal color is medium to light tan for a normally ridden machine. <PIC> A very white center electrode porcelain could indicate an intake track air leak or carburetion problem. Do not attempt to diagnose such problems yourself — take the machine to your dealer. Heat and deposits will cause any spark plug to slowly break down and erode over time, so periodic inspection is important. Consult your dealer before switching to a different spark plug type.", ["Manual34_134"]

"Two methods are available for memorizing communication channels. The first is Favorite Channel Memory, which lets you store up to five preferred channels for quick access. To set it up, press MENU, navigate to Favorite, select a favorite channel position, then choose the desired channel number. Once saved, press the FCR button repeatedly to cycle through your stored channels. <PIC> The second method is Manual Program with Fine Tuning, which lets you memorize a manually tuned channel after adjusting picture quality. After fine-tuning, press ENTER to memorize the channel. Note that if a finely tuned channel is memorized, its channel number turns yellow. To clear the fine tuning, re-program the channel via Auto Program or Manual Program. <PIC>", ["Manual35_22", "Manual35_23"]

"Common causes of poor TV signal reception include interference from automobile ignition systems, neon lamps, electric drills and other electrical appliances, which can cause picture flutter, drift, black spots or horizontal streaking. <PIC> Ghosts appear when the TV signal splits into a direct path and a reflected path off tall buildings or hills — adjusting the antenna direction may help. <PIC> Weak signals in fringe reception areas cause snow (small dots on screen), which may require a special antenna. <PIC> Strong random signals from cars or airplanes can also interfere with the TV signal, and a weak antenna signal is another common factor. For radio signal reception issues, interference can come from nearby equipment generating radio frequency energy. Reorienting or relocating the receiving antenna, increasing separation between the equipment and receiver, or connecting equipment to a different circuit outlet are recommended corrective measures.", ["television0_01", "television0_02", "television0_03"]

"To enable captions, press the CAPTION button and then press it again to select Off, Mode1, Mode2, Text1, or Text2. Press ENTER to exit. <PIC> Alternatively, press MENU, use the arrow buttons to select the Special menu, then select Caption, press the right arrow, and use up/down arrows to choose your preferred mode. Press MENU repeatedly to exit. <PIC> The TV remembers the last caption mode even when turned off. For on-screen text customization, the Text function provides information like weather, news, and stock topics. Note that not all stations offer text services even if they offer captioning. Poor signal may cause an empty black box to appear temporarily, which is normal.", ["Manual35_37", "Manual35_38"]

"Connect the DVD player's component outputs (Y, PB, PR for 480i) to the corresponding component input ports on the TV, and connect the audio outputs to the TV's audio L/R ports. <PIC> Once connected, turn on the TV and press the TV/VIDEO button on the remote or front panel to select COMPONENT mode, then turn on the DVD player. <PIC> For connecting to an audio system, use the DVD player's audio output jacks (L/R) and connect them to the audio input jacks on your audio system or receiver. Ensure all devices are powered off before making connections.", ["television0_04", "television0_05"]

"Read all instructions before use. Do not use outdoors or in poorly ventilated spaces. Keep the cord away from heat, sharp edges, and damp areas. Never pull the cord to disconnect — always pull the plug. Do not touch hot surfaces; use handles or knobs. Always place the appliance on a stable, flat, heat-resistant surface. <PIC> Never immerse the appliance in water or put it in a dishwasher. Disconnect from mains when not in use for long periods and before cleaning. Do not operate if the cord is damaged. Only use earthed power connections matching the voltage on the rating plate. "To activate or deactivate features on your electric toothbrush, you can do it from the handle or the app. From the handle, place the handle on the charging stand, then press and hold the power button for the required duration depending on the feature: - Pressure Sensor Feedback: up to 3 seconds (1 beep, SenseIQ indicator lights purple for 3 seconds) - Adaptive Intensity: up to 5 seconds (1 beep then 2 beeps, SenseIQ indicator lights purple for 3 seconds) - Brush head replacement reminder: up to 7 seconds (2 beeps then 3 beeps, replacement reminder indicator lights for 3 seconds) <PIC> Activated: battery indication blinks white 3 times + 3 tones low to high. Deactivated: battery indication blinks amber 3 times + 3 tones high to low. From the app, you can activate or deactivate Adaptive Intensity and Scrubbing Feedback. Note that deactivating the Pressure Sensor will also deactivate Adaptive Intensity.", ["Manual37_13"]

"To charge using the travel case, plug the USB cord into the travel case and into the USB wall adapter, then plug the wall adapter into an outlet. Place the toothbrush into the travel case. Make sure the travel case is placed on its side for better stability. <PIC> <PIC> To verify charging has started, the toothbrush will beep twice and the lights will illuminate in an upward motion. While charging, the battery indicator blinks in white. <PIC> Charging is complete when the battery light turns off (stops blinking).", ["Manual37_18", "Manual37_19", "Manual37_20"]
